# Finley and Nicks Awesome Site!
# Summary 
In order to improve the Comp110 class we suggest the addition of pre-class videos in order to explain complex concepts before class. This change would help students feel more prepared coming into class and hopefully increase success in course.
To analyze this, we graphed class data to see is such videos would be a neccessary and wanted resource.

## Conclusion
The data somewhat supports the idea that the course should  use pre-lecture videos, however, the results are not entirely conclusive. This data cannot directly show that videos would cause better understanding. The survey shows only opinions and relationships between variables, not an experiment. It is possible that students who already feel prepared still like the concept of pre-lecture videos, or that students who are struggling may need a different kind of support. A cost of using these videos is that instructors would more time to plan, record, and edit the videos. To reduce this issue, the videos should be short and focused only on previewing the hardest concepts.
